title: HAZ launches the Presseschau press review in Zurich
description: The Zurich group Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich (HAZ) began publishing the Presseschau, a review of press coverage on gay topics. Pink Cross took the publication over in 1997, continuing it as the LES.BI.SCHWULE PRESSESCHAU.

title: Gay men's group Wisch forms in Winterthur
description: A local association for gay men named Wisch was established in Winterthur, adding an organized meeting point to the city's community life.

title: Les Homos group founded in Schaffhausen
description: Community members in Schaffhausen founded the group Les Homos, giving the canton its own gay organization.

title: Petitpierre asks the Swiss government about equal rights for same-sex couples
description: Council of States member Gilles Petitpierre (FDP, Geneva) submitted a parliamentary question to the Federal Council asking what was being done about equal rights for same-sex couples. The query is regarded as the starting impulse for Switzerland's registered-partnership law.

title: Zurich marks 25 years of Stonewall with a two-month festival
description: An organizing committee in Zurich staged a festival program commemorating the 25th anniversary of the Stonewall riots. The celebrations revived the city's Christopher Street Day events, which had lapsed for several years.

title: Geneva holds a gay pride under the slogan 'Personne n'est parfait'
description: A gay pride march took place in Geneva on 25 June 1994, carrying the slogan 'Personne n'est parfait' (Nobody is perfect).

title: Swiss homophile organization SOH votes to dissolve
description: An extraordinary general assembly of the Schweizerische Organisation der Homophilen (SOH) resolved to dissolve the organization and asked its members to join the national association Pink Cross instead.

title: Italian-speaking gay community group CGST forms
description: The Comunità Gay Svizzera di Lingua Italiana (CGST) was created to serve gay men in Ticino and the Italian-speaking southern valleys of Graubünden.

title: Spazio Gay Ticino opens as a meeting place near Lugano
description: The Spazio Gay Ticino opened its doors in Massagno near Lugano, giving the region's gay community a dedicated meeting place.

title: Alpagai founded in the Valais
description: The regional group Alpagai was established to organize gay life in the canton of Valais.

title: Capricorn group forms for gays and lesbians in Graubünden
description: Capricorn, a group for gays and lesbians in the canton of Graubünden, came into being, organizing community life in the mountain canton.

title: Petition for equal rights for same-sex couples delivered with 85,181 signatures
description: Campaigners handed the petition 'Gleiche Rechte für gleichgeschlechtliche Paare' (Equal rights for same-sex couples), signed by 85,181 people, to the Federal Assembly at the Federal Palace in Bern. The petition became a key driver of the later registered-partnership legislation.

title: Community center Uferlos opens in Lucerne
description: Uferlos, a new community center for gays and lesbians, opened its doors in Lucerne on 1 April 1995.

title: New CSD association stages a Gay Parade in Zurich
description: The newly formed CSD organizing association in Zurich held a Gay Parade on 24 June 1995, continuing the revival of the city's pride events.

title: First public blessing ceremony for a gay couple held in Bern
description: Switzerland's first public church blessing of a gay couple took place in Bern on 8 July 1995.

title: Uferlos culture weeks bring queer arts festival to Bern
description: Gay and lesbian culture and festival weeks ran under the name Uferlos on the banks of the Aare in Bern from late July to mid-August 1995.

title: Network, an association of gay professionals, founded in Switzerland
description: Network, a Swiss association for gay men in leadership and professional positions, held its founding on 16 September 1995.

title: Swiss activist federation HACH dissolves in favor of Pink Cross
description: The umbrella of Swiss gay activist working groups, HACH, dissolved itself; the national association Pink Cross carried on as its successor organization.

title: Dialogai starts a group for gay retirees in Geneva
description: The Geneva organization Dialogai created 'Gays retraités', a group serving retired gay men.

title: Gay swim club Aquarius forms in Lausanne
description: A gay swimming club in Lausanne adopted the name 'Aquarius, club de natation', adding an organized sports group to the city's community.

title: Cantonal AIDS organization Empreinte established in Fribourg
description: Empreinte was set up as the cantonal AIDS support organization for Fribourg. From 1996 it cooperated with the local gay group Sarigai.

title: Aargay group forms in Baden
description: Gay men in Baden organized themselves as the Aargay, creating a community group for the canton of Aargau.

title: HAZ starts a group for gay men over 25 in Zurich
description: The Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich (HAZ) set up a new group for gay men over the age of 25.

title: HAZ offers a coming-out group for gay fathers
description: The Zurich organization HAZ began offering a coming-out support group aimed at gay fathers.

title: 'Lange Bank' protest on Bern's Bundesplatz over stalled petition
description: Activists staged the 'Lange Bank' action on the Bundesplatz in Bern because parliament had still not dealt with the equal-rights petition a full year after its submission.

title: VoGay founded in Lausanne
description: The organization VoGay was founded in Lausanne on 1 June 1996 to represent gay people in the canton of Vaud. Since November 2024 it has carried the name Voqueer.

title: Zurich celebrates Christopher Street Day as 'Züri Gay Fäscht'
description: Zurich's Christopher Street Day took place on 3 August 1996 under the name 'Züri Gay Fäscht', featuring a colorful parade and numerous parties.

title: Parents' initiative for equal rights for homosexuals founded
description: Parents of gay and lesbian children founded the 'Elterninitiative Gleiche Rechte für Homosexuelle' on 5 October 1996, organizing family support for equal rights in Switzerland.

title: Sarigay founded in Fribourg
description: The gay group Sarigay was founded in Fribourg on 7 October 1996, giving the canton its own community organization.

title: Adamin, the Swiss association of gay pastoral workers, founded
description: Adamin, an association bringing together gay pastoral care workers across Switzerland, came into being on 15 October 1996.

title: MediGay forms for gay and lesbian health professionals
description: An informal circle of gay medical professionals reorganized in December 1996 as MediGay, an association for gays and lesbians in medical and nursing professions. From 2008 it operated as a specialist group of Pink Cross.

title: HAZ over-25 group takes the name Spätzünder
description: The HAZ group for men over 25 in Zurich renamed itself 'Spätzünder' (late bloomers).

title: First Mister Gay Switzerland elected
description: Switzerland elected its first Mister Gay Switzerland, a title intended to give the gay community a public representative.

title: Club Aera opens in Zurich
description: The Aera club opened in Zurich, initially without a license and legally from 1998. For years its Saturday nights were reserved for gay men under the motto 'No dick - no entry'.

title: Teachers' association VHELS transfers its work to Pink Cross
description: The Swiss association of homosexual educators and teachers (VHELS) handed its activities over to Pink Cross on 24 May 1997 and dissolved as an independent organization.

title: 6,000 rally in Bern for constitutional protection against discrimination
description: A national lesbian and gay demonstration drew 6,000 people to the Bundesplatz in Bern on 31 May 1997. Under a motto punning on being 'in good constitution', the rally demanded explicit protection against discrimination in the new Swiss federal constitution.

title: First Romandie-wide Lesbian & Gay Pride held in Geneva
description: Geneva hosted the first Lesbian & Gay Pride covering all of French-speaking Switzerland on 5 July 1997.

title: Zurich CSD parade goes apolitical, drawing criticism
description: The 1997 Christopher Street Day in Zurich featured a parade without political content but many well-attended parties. The national organizations Pink Cross and LOS publicly objected to the depoliticized format.

title: HAZ newsletter schwall begins regular publication
description: 'schwall', the new information bulletin of the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich (HAZ), started appearing on a regular basis in September 1997.

title: Queersicht film festival debuts in Bern
description: Bern held its first lesbian-gay film festival under the name Queersicht from 30 October to 9 November 1997. The festival has been staged annually ever since.

title: National parents and friends organization FELS founded in Olten
description: FELS, the national organization of friends and parents of lesbians and gays, emerged from the earlier parents' initiative for equal rights and was constituted as a national association in Olten on 1 November 1997.

title: PinkRail founded for queer public-transport employees
description: PinkRail, an association of lesbian and gay employees in Swiss public transport, held its founding assembly on 22 November 1997.

title: Pink Cross and LOS submit draft partnership-law proposals
description: The national organizations Pink Cross and LOS filed two draft variants of a law on equal rights for same-sex couples with the Swiss Federal Department of Justice and Police, advancing the legislative process toward registered partnership.

title: Queeramnesty Schweiz forms within Amnesty International
description: A Swiss Amnesty International group dedicated to the rights of queer people was founded under the name Queeramnesty Schweiz.

title: Gay swim club H2O starts in Geneva
description: H2O club de natation, a swimming club for gay men, was established in Geneva.

title: Cranberry Bar opens in Zurich
description: The Cranberry Bar opened in Zurich and quickly established itself as a meeting spot for gays and lesbians.

title: LesBiSchwul group forms in Zug
description: The group LesBiSchwul Zug formed, organizing lesbian, bisexual and gay community life in the canton of Zug.

title: Rosa Telefon counseling line set up in Graubünden
description: A telephone counseling service named Rosa Telefon was established in the canton of Graubünden for gay and lesbian callers.

title: Holocaust Fund supports a gay survivor, recognizing gays as victim group
description: Acting on a request by Pink Cross, the Swiss Holocaust Fund granted support to a gay Holocaust survivor for the first time on 21 January 1998. The decision acknowledged gay men as a victim group of Nazi persecution.
